scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein); build_insns 42 vs target 43

sessions.lever: remove $5 pin + asm barrier; named `partial` intermediate; swept addition-order + base-position variants
swept 10 additional structural variants under instrumented codegen comparison (objdump tail of sandbox .o vs target). NONE produced var_v0 -> $v0; all stayed at build_insns 42 vs target 43.
variants_ruled_out: var_v0 declared FIRST (lower LUID) — same allocation
remove `a3` intermediate (inline a3 def in `base =`) — same allocation
`var_v0 += base; return var_v0 + lookup + 0x12C;` — DIFFERENT alloc (a2->$a1, base->$a2, var_v0->$v1), still 42 insns, partial-add still in lh delay slot
explicit `s32 lookup = ...;` after `s32 partial = base+var_v0;` — same alloc as canonical candidate

`var_v0 += base; var_v0 += lookup; var_v0 += 0x12C; return var_v0;` (var_v0 as accumulator/return-thread) — DIFFERENT alloc (base->$a2, var_v0->$v1, lookup->$v0), still 42 insns, partial fills lh delay slot
`s32 partial = a3 * 40;` then `partial += var_v0;` (SAME var split-init) — DIFFERENT alloc (partial->$a2, var_v0->$v1), still 42 insns, partial-add in lh delay slot
`return (base + lookup + 0x12C) + var_v0;` (var_v0 as LAST addend) — DIFFERENT alloc; addiu 0x12C fills lh delay slot, still 42 insns
`register s32 var_v0;` (storage-class hint) — same alloc as candidate; NOTE: also counts as a residual non-pin register hint, dropped

reviewer.evidence: Candidate is clean (0 rules, 0 cheat-asm; pin + barrier removed). Not byte-identical (full build SHA1 mismatch 650213f4...) so NOT committed to src. The residual is a register-allocation tie, not a cheat.

remaining_gap.summary: Target holds `base` (a3*40) in $a1 and `var_v0` in $v0 throughout, then `addu a1,a1,v0` (partial) BEFORE the final `lh` (so the lh's delay slot is a nop). My build allocates base->$a3 (var_v0's test `lh a1,94(a0)` claims $a1 first), so the partial-add fills the lh delay slot instead (build 1 insn SHORT, 42 vs 43). It's a base<->var_v0 register swap + a delay-slot-fill consequence.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD byte-matches only via the $5 pin + asm barrier (both cheat-asm). No pure-C lever found to push base into $a1 / var_v0 into $v0.

next_hypotheses: Get var_v0 to live in $v0 (target) instead of $a1: the `(var_v0 = *(s16*)(arg0+0x5E))` test-assignment claims $a1. Try a block-local for the test value distinct from var_v0's result so var_v0's pseudo prefers $v0 — but a naive split scored 14 (the test value IS reused as the discarded load). Needs a form where the test reads into $v0 and is discarded, AND var_v0 results also land in $v0, freeing $a1 for base.

decomp-permuter (40-insn fn, pure RA/scheduling residual — its specialty). Verify any find with the FULL build SHA1 (the masked sandbox shows 10 regardless of the a1/a3 choice; full build proves it — confirmed mismatch 650213f4).
If pure-C plateaus, canonical-asm auth or documented plateau. Same RA-coupling class as this batch's other masked-10 backlog items.

rejected_forms.form: drop the `var_v0 =` test-assignment (test the load directly)
rejected_forms.reason: Worse: target reuses the loaded value, so removing the assignment changes the load. Keep `(var_v0 = *(s16*)(arg0+0x5E))`.

rejected_forms.form: compute base = a3*40 AFTER the if/else / inline at use
rejected_forms.reason: Much worse (build 39): moves the multiply past the load. base must be computed early (as target does).

rejected_forms.form: base += var_v0 in place / partial += 0x12C / group const
rejected_forms.reason: Addition reordering does not force partial before the load; all worse than the named-partial floor 10.

rejected_forms.form: structural variants of accumulator/split-init/declaration-order (2026-06-22 session, 10 forms)
rejected_forms.reason: All produce masked floor 10 (= candidate.c floor) but differ in WHICH physical register hosts var_v0. NONE produce var_v0 -> $v0. GCC 2.7.2's allocator routes var_v0 to $a1 / $v1 / $v0(via accumulator) depending on form; even when var_v0 lands in $v0 (full-accumulator variant), the partial-add (`addu` of base+var_v0) doesn't displace the lh delay slot fill because base lives in a different reg (e.g. $a2), so the scheduler still fills the lh's slot with the partial-add. The wall isn't 'var_v0 in $v0' — it's the FULL coupling: var_v0 in $v0 AND base in $a1 simultaneously, so that `addu $a1, $a1, $v0` is FORCED before the `lw $v0, 0($a0)` (otherwise the lw clobbers live var_v0).
